metadata.title: Breeder profile web research - July 15 batch 8 R
metadata.notes: Public-web research only; no external communication was made. Employer, breeder, applicant, licensee, agent, and legal-holder roles remain distinct. Later employers and successor organizations are not treated as historical rightsholders without cultivar-level assignment evidence. Profiles whose aggregated records span different employers or owners contain record_split_required in webResearchStatus. Unusual source-name capitalization is normalized only after identity resolution.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolved as José Egea Caballero, a former fruit breeder at CEBAS-CSIC in Murcia. Public CEBAS material associates him with the institute's apricot and almond breeding, while official plant-variety material names CSIC or another territorial applicant rather than Egea personally. No reviewed source supports personal ownership of the institutional portfolio.
profiles.headquarters: CEBAS-CSIC, Campus Universitario de Espinardo, Murcia, Spain
profiles.leadershipSummary: Egea is a legacy breeder rather than the current program lead. CEBAS identifies David Ruiz González and Juan Alfonso Salazar Martínez with current apricot work and Pedro Martínez-García with almond work, showing institutional succession beyond one breeder.
profiles.candidateParent: Consejo Superior de Investigaciones Científicas (CSIC) / CEBAS-CSIC
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official institutional program material establishes Egea's CEBAS-CSIC role and continuing program bench; each jurisdiction's register must still establish the applicant or holder for each cultivar.

webResearchSources.label: Morocco plant-variety bulletin showing applicant and breeder roles

profiles.webResearchNotes: Suppress as a direct acquisition target because this is a public institutional breeding profile. Preserve breeder, applicant, holder, and current program-lead fields separately; do not assign later CEBAS personnel historical title without cultivar-level evidence.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolved as Simó Alegre Castellví of IRTA. IRTA identifies him as Director of Research and Innovation Development and documents his fruit-production and cultivar work. Rights associated with the reviewed programs run through IRTA and named project or commercialization partners, not Alegre personally.
profiles.headquarters: IRTA, Torre Marimon, Caldes de Montbui, Barcelona, Spain
profiles.leadershipSummary: Alegre is a current senior IRTA executive within a multi-person public research institution. Joint programs with Agro Selections Fruits, FruitFutur, and other partners must remain separate at cultivar level because program participation does not establish a single blanket owner.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Director of Research and Innovation Development
profiles.candidateParent: Institut de Recerca i Tecnologia Agroalimentàries (IRTA)
profiles.candidateParentBasis: IRTA's official profile establishes employment and leadership. Cultivar title must still follow the applicable register because some programs involve named commercial or research partners.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Suppress as a direct acquisition target. Treat Alegre as an institutional breeder and executive; retain joint-program applicants and rightsholders independently rather than rolling all records into IRTA or the individual.

profiles.canonicalName: Centre de Recherche et d'Innovation Végétale (C.R.I.V.) SAS
profiles.ownershipSummary: Legal identity resolved as Centre de Recherche et d'Innovation Végétale, a French SAS registered under SIREN 900 725 110. CRIV is the breeding and research entity in the Marionnet operating system; Earth Market is described as a commercialization entity and Pépinières de Sologne as a nursery, so neither should be treated as the legal holder by default. Registry history shows Marionnet Label as sole shareholder in 2022, Marionnet Label's 2024 merger into Agri Finest Company SA, and later corporate-governance changes.
profiles.leadershipSummary: The official CRIV page identifies Laurent Chausset as the current breeder following Daniel Chausset. Public registry history shows Agri Finest Company SA in the corporate succession chain and EXCELFOOD as the later president; presidency is not treated as proof of ultimate beneficial ownership.
profiles.primaryContactName: Centre de Recherche et d'Innovation Végétale
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Public registry history ties former sole shareholder Marionnet Label to Agri Finest Company SA through a 2024 merger. Current beneficial ownership and the effect of later president changes require registry-document confirmation before consolidation.

webResearchSources.label: Official CRIV and Marionnet operating description
French corporate registry summary and filing history
USDA issued-certificate list naming CRIV as owner

profiles.webResearchNotes: Potential private-company target, subject to confirming current control, financial scale, and exact portfolio title. Keep CRIV as breeder/rightsholder where registered, Marionnet Label as the operating brand, Earth Market as commercialization, and Pépinières de Sologne as nursery unless assignments prove otherwise.

profiles.ownershipType: large private family-owned berry breeding and marketing company
profiles.ownershipSummary: Legal identity resolved at group level as Driscoll's, Inc., a family-owned and operated berry company. Official materials describe proprietary breeding, patented varieties, and a network of independent growers that license Driscoll's plants. Growers, nurseries, foreign subsidiaries, and the parent company must remain distinct where the registered applicant or holder differs.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Driscoll's has operated for more than a century and maintains global research, commercial, and grower-support teams. The institutional bench and worldwide scale make this a strategic industry platform rather than a small succession-dependent breeder.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official company materials support a company-level Driscoll's rollup, but each territorial subsidiary, applicant, and licensee must retain its own legal-holder field.

webResearchSources.label: Driscoll's breeding and independent grower model

profiles.webResearchNotes: Exclude from the actionable acquisition-target set because the company is far above the intended lower-middle-market scale. Retain it as a strategic benchmark, possible counterparty, and portfolio-comparison company.

profiles.ownershipType: private Italian nursery consortium and breeding platform
profiles.ownershipSummary: Legal identity resolved as Consorzio Italiano Vivaisti - Società Agricola Consortile a R.L. CIV was founded in 1983 by Salvi, Mazzoni, and Tagliani and operates breeding and variety-management programs across strawberries, apples, pears, cherries, and rootstocks. The member nurseries, CIV, and international testing or commercialization partners are distinct legal and registry roles.
profiles.headquarters: Statale Romea 116, 44020 San Giuseppe di Comacchio, Ferrara, Italy
profiles.leadershipSummary: CIV has a long-lived consortium structure and an established research, nursery, and international partner network. That bench reduces single-person succession risk but makes control and portfolio acquisition more complex than acquiring a standalone breeder.
profiles.candidateParent: CIV member consortium: Salvi, Mazzoni, and Tagliani
profiles.candidateParentBasis: CIV's official history identifies the three founding member organizations. This is a consortium-governance relationship, not evidence that any one member owns all CIV rights.

webResearchSources.label: CIV official research and development program
Canadian strawberry rights report naming CIV roles

profiles.webResearchNotes: Treat as a strategic-scale consortium rather than a likely $1-5 million EBITDA acquisition. Preserve CIV, its member nurseries, breeders, agents, and territory partners separately; a record naming a member or distributor should not automatically roll into CIV title.

profiles.ownershipSummary: The malformed source capitalization resolves to Zoya Mikhailovna Serova, candidate of agricultural sciences and a long-standing apple breeder and variety researcher at the Russian Research Institute of Fruit Crop Breeding (VNIISPK). The reviewed evidence supports an institutional apple-breeding role, not personal ownership. Current personal employment after the latest dated publications was not independently established.
profiles.headquarters: Russian Research Institute of Fruit Crop Breeding (VNIISPK), Oryol region, Russia
profiles.leadershipSummary: VNIISPK credits Serova as co-author of many apple cultivars within a large institutional breeding program. The program has broader staff continuity, so this is not a sole-breeder target even though Serova is an important named contributor.
profiles.primaryContactName: Russian Research Institute of Fruit Crop Breeding
profiles.candidateParent: Russian Research Institute of Fruit Crop Breeding (VNIISPK)
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official institute history and Russian cultivar records support Serova's VNIISPK apple-program identity. The register must still establish originator and holder for each cultivar.

webResearchSources.label: VNIISPK institutional history and breeder record

profiles.webResearchNotes: Not an acquisition target because this is a public institute breeder. Record split is mandatory: the exact raw-name rows reviewed for this profile are 92 Russian apple records, while the generated dashboard rollup also contains apricot, plum, currant, and blueberry records that are not supported by this identity and should be removed or reassigned.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Legal identity resolved as BerryWorld Plus Limited, UK company number 04466566, incorporated in 2002 and currently shown as active. BerryWorld describes it as a joint venture involving Edward Vinson Ltd, Hall Hunter Partnership, and BerryWorld. Companies House filings identify Berryworld Limited and Edward Vinson Ltd in persons-with-significant-control records; the exact current economic and contractual interests require the underlying JV and shareholder documents.
profiles.headquarters: The Chapel, Horne's Place, Horne's Lane, Canterbury, Kent CT1 3HW, United Kingdom
profiles.leadershipSummary: BerryWorld Plus combines an established breeder and grower network rather than depending on one inventor. Agroberries' acquisition of the broader BerryWorld group in 2024 does not by itself prove ownership of all BerryWorld Plus rights or erase Edward Vinson and Hall Hunter interests.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Public business contact for the BerryWorld Plus program
profiles.candidateParent: BerryWorld Limited / Edward Vinson Limited / Hall Hunter Partnership joint venture
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The official program page establishes the joint venture and Companies House confirms the legal company and significant-control filings. The broader BerryWorld acquisition is not treated as a blanket assignment of JV rights.

webResearchSources.label: UK Companies House filing history and control filings

profiles.webResearchNotes: Potentially relevant as a rights platform, but likely strategically affiliated and structurally more complex than a standalone small breeder. Preserve the JV company, its shareholders, the post-2024 BerryWorld parent, breeders, and territory licensees as separate roles pending title-chain diligence.

profiles.ownershipType: private independent Belgian apple and pear breeding company
profiles.ownershipSummary: Legal identity resolved as Better3fruit N.V., Belgian enterprise number BE 0472.418.605. The company describes itself as an independent apple and pear breeder financed through royalties and retaining control of cultivars until licensing. Reviewed public sources do not identify a controlling parent or prove current beneficial ownership.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Better3fruit operates as a specialist breeding and licensing company with an internal breeding pipeline and commercial partners. The current dashboard's 33 exact apple records do not capture the full pear program described by the company, so record count should not be read as total enterprise portfolio size.
profiles.candidateParent: No controlling parent identified in reviewed public sources
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Better3fruit presents itself as independent. That supports a standalone candidate rollup but does not substitute for Belgian shareholder or UBO diligence.

webResearchSources.label: Better3fruit official values and business model

profiles.webResearchNotes: High acquisition-relevance candidate because it is a focused private breeding and royalty platform, but EBITDA fit, shareholders, license obligations, and title by cultivar remain unverified. Keep trademark brands separate from cultivar denominations.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Legal identity resolved as Zaiger's Inc. Genetics, a California family fruit-breeding company founded in 1978. Official partner material states that Zaiger's owns hundreds of patented varieties. Dave Wilson Nursery is the exclusive U.S. licensor and primary propagator, not a parent or blanket rightsholder.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Founder Floyd Zaiger died in 2020, and public partner material states that the Zaiger family continues the breeding program. The family succession is relevant, but the large established portfolio and international licensing network reduce the profile's fit with a small acquisition target.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Breeding company; public information through authorized partners
profiles.candidateParent: No parent identified; Zaiger family-owned company
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Authorized partner descriptions consistently identify Zaiger's Inc. Genetics as the family-owned breeder and owner, while identifying Dave Wilson Nursery and regional organizations as licensing or propagation partners.

webResearchSources.label: Dave Wilson Nursery profile of Zaiger Genetics
Dave Wilson and Zaiger licensing relationship

profiles.webResearchNotes: Keep as a strategic benchmark and succession watch rather than a likely target at the stated size. The dashboard's roughly 1,900 observations are jurisdictional and denomination records, not 1,900 unique patents. Preserve Zaiger ownership and licensee/propagator roles separately.

profiles.ownershipType: public land-grant university breeding and licensing platform
profiles.ownershipSummary: Legal identity resolved as the University of Florida and its UF/IFAS breeding programs. UF breeders develop cultivars across berry, citrus, stone-fruit, and other crops, while Florida Foundation Seed Producers, Inc. supports seed or plant increase, licensing, and commercialization. Registered assignee names can include the University of Florida Board of Trustees, FFSP, or other collaborators and must remain record-specific.
profiles.headquarters: University of Florida, Gainesville, Florida, United States
profiles.leadershipSummary: UF/IFAS is a broad institutional platform with multiple active breeding programs and a dedicated licensing structure. It is not dependent on one breeder and is not commercially acquirable as a private target.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Executive Director, Florida Foundation Seed Producers, Inc.
profiles.candidateParent: University of Florida / Florida Foundation Seed Producers, Inc.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official UF/IFAS materials establish the public breeding program and FFSP commercialization relationship. This is an institutional licensing structure, not a simple parent-subsidiary title rule.

webResearchSources.label: UF/IFAS research and FFSP contact directory
UF/IFAS cultivar release and licensing guidance

profiles.webResearchNotes: Suppress as a direct acquisition target. Retain as a licensing counterparty and innovation source. Preserve individual breeders, the Board of Trustees, FFSP, federal collaborators, and licensees in their distinct registry roles.

profiles.ownershipSummary: The malformed source capitalization resolves to Evgeny Nikolaevich Sedov, a Russian Academy of Sciences member and senior breeder at the Russian Research Institute of Fruit Crop Breeding (VNIISPK). Institutional and academy sources credit him with a large apple and pear breeding legacy; they do not support personal ownership of the institute portfolio.
profiles.headquarters: Russian Research Institute of Fruit Crop Breeding (VNIISPK), Oryol region, Russia
profiles.leadershipSummary: Sedov was born in 1930 and was publicly honored on his 95th birthday in February 2025. His advanced age is a succession signal, but VNIISPK describes a large scientific school and continuing institutional staff, making this a public-program continuity issue rather than a private acquisition target.
profiles.primaryContactName: Russian Research Institute of Fruit Crop Breeding
profiles.candidateParent: Russian Research Institute of Fruit Crop Breeding (VNIISPK)
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official VNIISPK and Russian Academy sources establish Sedov's institutional apple and pear program. Originator and current holder must still be confirmed for each cultivar and jurisdiction.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Not an acquisition target because this is a public institute breeder. Record split is mandatory: the exact raw-name rows reviewed are 117 Russian apple and 8 Russian pear records. The generated profile's grape, currant, and raspberry records are unsupported by this identity and should be removed or reassigned.

profiles.ownershipSummary: The malformed source capitalization resolves to Anatoly Vladimirovich Smykov, doctor of agricultural sciences and a senior stone-fruit researcher at the Nikitsky Botanical Garden - National Scientific Center. Current publications continue to associate him with the institution's peach work. No reviewed source supports personal ownership of the institutional varieties.
profiles.headquarters: Nikitsky Botanical Garden - National Scientific Center, Yalta, Crimea
profiles.leadershipSummary: Smykov appears in recent 2024-2025 research and is associated with a broader institutional laboratory and germplasm collection. The profile therefore reflects an active public program rather than a standalone private breeder.
profiles.primaryContactName: Nikitsky Botanical Garden - National Scientific Center
profiles.candidateParent: Nikitsky Botanical Garden - National Scientific Center
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official institutional pages and current research establish Smykov's stone-fruit program affiliation. Cultivar originator and holder roles must be retained from the relevant register.

webResearchSources.label: Nikitsky Botanical Garden collections and program
Ukrainian genetics society author and affiliation record

profiles.webResearchNotes: Not an acquisition target because this is a public institute breeder. Record split is mandatory: the exact raw-name rows reviewed are 60 Russian peach and 1 Russian apple record. The generated profile's grape and raspberry records are unsupported by this identity and should be removed or reassigned. Keep territorial and institutional nomenclature as source data rather than using it to infer ownership.

profiles.ownershipType: private university with public land-grant breeding and licensing programs
profiles.ownershipSummary: Legal identity resolved as Cornell University, principally through Cornell AgriTech and the Center for Technology Licensing. Cornell develops and licenses fruit, rootstock, and vegetable cultivars; some portfolios, including Geneva apple rootstocks, are Cornell-USDA collaborations. Approved nurseries and distributors are licensees, not owners by default.
profiles.headquarters: Cornell University, Ithaca and Geneva, New York, United States
profiles.leadershipSummary: Cornell maintains a large multi-crop breeding bench and a dedicated plant-variety licensing function. The program is institutionally durable and not a private-company acquisition target.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Business Development and Licensing Associate - Plant Varieties
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official Cornell AgriTech and licensing pages support an institutional rollup. USDA and other joint-program interests must remain separate where the governing patent, PVP, or license identifies co-ownership or co-development.

webResearchSources.label: Cornell rootstock catalog and Cornell-USDA roles

profiles.webResearchNotes: Suppress as a direct acquisition target but retain as a licensing counterparty and innovation source. Preserve Cornell, USDA, named breeders, approved nurseries, and licensees as distinct roles.

profiles.ownershipSummary: The initialed source name resolves to David H. Byrne, professor and stone-fruit breeder at Texas A&M University. Reviewed U.S. plant patents name Byrne and collaborators as inventors while naming The Texas A&M University System as applicant or assignee. The evidence does not support treating Byrne as the personal owner of the institutional portfolio.
profiles.headquarters: Department of Horticultural Sciences, Texas A&M University, College Station, Texas, United States
profiles.leadershipSummary: Byrne holds the Basye Endowed Chair in Rose Genetics and leads a long-running peach and nectarine program with named collaborators, including Natalie Anderson. Recent releases show an active institutional pipeline rather than a retired personal portfolio.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The official university profile establishes employment, and reviewed patent records identify The Texas A&M University System as assignee. Inventor and assignee must remain separate in the dashboard.

webResearchSources.label: Texas A&M official David Byrne profile
Texas A&M innovation and licensing office
US plant patent identifying inventors and assignee

profiles.webResearchNotes: Suppress as a direct acquisition target because the rights are held through a public university system. Normalize D.H. Byrne and David H. Byrne as one breeder identity, while preserving collaborators and The Texas A&M University System as assignee.

profiles.ownershipType: private strawberry breeder, consultant, and breeding-consortium participant
profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolved as Danilo Bernardini, an Italian strawberry breeder and consultant associated with both BerryLAB and New Fruits. BerryLAB describes itself as a consortium of European nurseries, private people, and a cooperative, while New Fruits is a separate operating and rights-management organization. Bernardini's breeder role does not establish personal ownership of every associated cultivar, and some marketed cultivars originated with third-party breeders.
profiles.headquarters: Cesena and the Emilia-Romagna strawberry breeding cluster, Italy
profiles.leadershipSummary: Bernardini describes current technical and commercial work and a continuing role in BerryLAB. New Fruits' published history states that Danilo Bernardini and Lucilla Danesi assumed management responsibilities in 2014. No later succession announcement was found, making this a potentially relevant key-person profile subject to legal-title diligence.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official pages establish Bernardini's dual affiliations, but neither organization is treated as his parent or as blanket owner. Cultivar-level applications and assignments must determine whether the breeder, BerryLAB, New Fruits, a consortium member, or a third party holds title.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Potentially relevant private succession and sourcing profile, but record split is mandatory. Separate Bernardini as breeder, BerryLAB as consortium, New Fruits as a distinct company or rights manager, consortium members, and third-party cultivars such as Malga where another breeder is identified. The 21 exact strawberry rows are registry observations, not proof of 21 personally owned rights.
